zh.lifeSummary: 當Loretta FitzRoy於大約1228年出生在英國英格蘭肯特時，她的父親Baron Richard Fitzroy39歲，母親Rohese de Dover11226歲。她於1248年6月7日與William Marmion 2nd Baron of Winteringham結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1275年在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾文特林加姆享年49歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾文特林加姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Loretta FitzRoy于大约1228年出生在英国英格兰肯特时，她的父亲Baron Richard Fitzroy39岁，母亲Rohese de Dover11226岁。她于1248年6月7日与William Marmion 2nd Baron of Winteringham结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于大约1275年在英国英格兰林可爾史爾文特林加姆享年49岁，埋葬在英国英格兰林可爾史爾文特林加姆。

nameMeanings.name: FitzRoy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), ‘son of Herbert’.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), ‘son of Henry’. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Éinrí. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), ‘son of Simon or Sigmund’. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac Síomóin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; ‘son of Gibbon’. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as ‘the White Knights’ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Loretta
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Lauretta  , normally borne by Roman Catholics, among whom it is associated with   Loreto .

variants.name: Lauretta
meanings: diminutive form of   Laura  , also sometimes used in the English-speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: When Loretta FitzRoy was born about 1228, in Kent, England, United Kingdom, her father, Baron Richard Fitzroy, was 39 and her mother, Rohese de Dover, was 11226. She married William Marmion 2nd Baron of Winteringham on 7 June 1248. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died about 1275, in Winteringham, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 49, and was buried in Winteringham, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Loretta FitzRoy est née environ 1228, à Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Baron Richard Fitzroy, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Rohese de Dover, avait 11226 ans. Elle a épousé William Marmion 2nd Baron of Winteringham le 7 juin 1248. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée environ 1275, à Winteringham, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterrée à Winteringham, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Loretta FitzRoy um 1228 in Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Baron Richard Fitzroy 39 und ihre Mutter Rohese de Dover 11226. Sie hat am 7. Juni 1248 William Marmion 2nd Baron of Winteringham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb um 1275 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Winteringham, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Winteringham, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Loretta FitzRoy è nata il circa 1228 nel Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Baron Richard Fitzroy, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Rohese de Dover, aveva 11226 anni. Lei sposato William Marmion 2nd Baron of Winteringham il 7 giugno 1248. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì circa 1275, nel Winteringham, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 49 anni, e fu sepolta a Winteringham, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Loretta FitzRoyが約1228年にイギリスイギリスケントで生まれた時，父Baron Richard Fitzroyは39歳，母Rohese de Doverは11226歳であった。1248年6月7日にWilliam Marmion 2nd Baron of Winteringhamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は約1275年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアウィンテリンガムで49歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアウィンテリンガムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Loretta FitzRoy(이)가 대략 1228년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Baron Richard Fitzroy(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Rohese de Dover(은)는 11226세였다. 그녀는 1248년 6월 7일 William Marmion 2nd Baron of Winteringham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1275년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 윈테링함에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 윈테링함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Loretta FitzRoy nasceu aproximadamente 1228, em Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Baron Richard Fitzroy, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Rohese de Dover, tinha 11226 anos. Ela casou-se com William Marmion 2nd Baron of Winteringham em 7 de junho de 1248. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1275, em Winteringham, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 49 anos, e foi sepultada em Winteringham, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Loretta FitzRoy родилась около 1228 г. в местности кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Baron Richard Fitzroy было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Rohese de Dover, 11226. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Marmion 2nd Baron of Winteringham 7 июня 1248 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла около 1275 г. в местности винтирингхам, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 49 лет и была похоронена в местности винтирингхам, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Loretta FitzRoy nació aproximadamente 1228, en Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Baron Richard Fitzroy, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Rohese de Dover, tenía 11226 años. Se casó con William Marmion 2nd Baron of Winteringham el 7 de junio de 1248. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió aproximadamente 1275, en Winteringham, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 49 años, y fue sepultada en Winteringham, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
